description: Extract the dispatch-optimizer idea into a production-grade library ecosystem: spec, client/serializer, optimizer, plugins, tools, orchestrator, and CLI
problem: The dispatch-optimizer (docs/plan/dispatch-optimizer) is a 3,132-line proof-of-concept in a docs directory with no package boundary, no tests, no DI, stubbed features, and no consumer-facing surface. It cannot be consumed as a library, dispatched by the orchestrator, or integrated with agent-mcp in production.
approach: Decompose into 7 layered packages + 1 CLI app following the existing monorepo architecture. The key structural decisions: (1) serialization adapter pattern (IDagSerializer) keeps the client unaware of storage; (2) DagClient is the single CRUD authority — agents never read dag.json raw; (3) optimizer is pure computation with injected dependencies; (4) plugins (IO, gitnexus) enrich snapshots post-hoc; (5) MCP tools wrap the client for agent-safe dag manipulation.

spec-types.description: @adhd/dispatch-spec ships: TypeScript types (DagJson, DagSnapshot, DispatchUnit, Shape, Operation, Milestone, DispatchLog, all sub-types) from the current types.ts; JSON Schema documents for dag.json v4 and dag-snapshot.json; schema_version field enforcement; a migrateDag() function that accepts (fromVersion, toVersion, dag) and returns the upgraded dag
spec-types.rationale: Types are the data contract. Every other package imports from @adhd/dispatch-spec. Must be platform:shared, zero dependencies, no I/O. The existing types.ts (660 lines) is the source; extract into a proper Nx package with index.ts barrel export. JSON Schema documents enable non-TS consumers (Python orchestrator, Go agent) to validate dag.json without a TS runtime.
spec-types.guard: npx nx build dispatch-spec && npx nx typecheck dispatch-spec

spec-validate.description: @adhd/dispatch-spec exports: validateDagJson(dag) → { valid, errors[] } covering: cycle detection in depends_on, no orphan ops (operation.milestone must exist), D-07 invariant (eligible implies pending==null), guard op id convention (<slug>.guard), shape.kind valid enum, all required fields present; validateSnapshot(snap) → { valid, errors[] } covering: all derived fields populated, no null waves, status is valid enum, pairwise_overlap keys are sorted slug pairs, open_questions have valid blocking references
spec-validate.rationale: Validation is pure structural logic — no I/O, no filesystem. Lives in spec alongside types because validation is part of the data contract: a dag.json that passes validateDagJson() is a valid input to any compliant implementation. The current validate.ts (325 lines) is a partial implementation; this milestone completes it.

client-core.description: @adhd/dispatch-client ships: (1) IDagSerializer interface — read(), write(), appendDispatchLog(), onExternalWrite?() — the serialization adapter contract; (2) IDagClient interface — milestone_add/get/list/update/delete, operation_add/get/list/update/delete, pending_set/clear, dispatch_log_append/get, field_get/set, full_dag(), validate(), create(); (3) DagClient(options: { serializer, validate }) implementation — validates after every mutation, enforces that operations reference existing milestones, dispatch_log_append is append-only, full_dag() returns the cached in-memory copy; (4) createDagClient() factory function
client-core.rationale: DagClient is the single CRUD authority. No agent, CLI, or orchestrator reads dag.json raw — they call DagClient methods. The adapter pattern (IDagSerializer) is modeled on the existing HostAdapter pattern from @adhd/apigen-gateway (factory function → object literal implementing interface). The client itself is platform:shared (no I/O — the serializer does I/O). Validation injection keeps the client decoupled from schema version: pass in the validator from @adhd/dispatch-spec.
client-core.guard: npx nx build dispatch-client && npx nx typecheck dispatch-client && npx nx test dispatch-client

serializer-json.description: @adhd/dispatch-serializer-json ships: createJsonFileSerializer(filePath) → IDagSerializer. Atomic writes via temp-file + rename (same as current io.ts). read() reads and parses JSON (returns null if file doesn't exist, which triggers client.create()). appendDispatchLog() reads, appends, writes atomically. onExternalWrite() uses fs.watch. Platform: node (uses fs, path).
serializer-json.rationale: This is the canonical local serializer — dag.json on disk. Extracted from the current io.ts (114 lines). By implementing IDagSerializer, it's swappable: the orchestrator can use this locally, a remote version for server-managed plans, or a SQLite serializer for embedded use.
serializer-json.guard: npx nx build dispatch-serializer-json && npx nx test dispatch-serializer-json

optimizer-core.description: @adhd/dispatch-optimizer ships: (1) IOptimizerDeps interface — { bPerTier, contextWindowPerTier, fileSizes?, dispatchLog? } — injected dependencies, none required beyond bPerTier and contextWindow (cold-start defaults); (2) snapshot(dag: DagJson, deps: IOptimizerDeps): DagSnapshot — pure computation, no I/O, ported from compiler.ts §snapshot(); (3) optimize(snapshot: DagSnapshot, deps: IOptimizerDeps): DispatchUnit[] — pure computation, ported from compiler.ts §optimize() with all 4 algorithms; (4) remove snapshotWithDag() — the dag-provider/effort injection goes into the orchestrator, not the optimizer. Platform: shared. Dependencies: @adhd/dispatch-spec only.
optimizer-core.rationale: The optimizer is the crown jewel — the 4-algorithm cascade (Bitmask DP, Tree DP, SA, HLFET) with automatic DAG structure detection. Must be pure computation: no I/O, no side effects, no agent-mcp knowledge. The current compiler.ts (2,033 lines) calls fs.statSync internally — this must be replaced with injected fileSizes() so the optimizer works with any byte provider (real FS, virtual FS, SQLite blob store, zero for cold-start).
optimizer-core.guard: npx nx build dispatch-optimizer && npx nx typecheck dispatch-optimizer && npx nx test dispatch-optimizer

plugin-io.description: @adhd/dispatch-plugin-io ships: createIOPlugin(rootDir: string) → { fileSizes(paths: string[]): Map<string, number>, readFiles(paths: string[]): Map<string, string> }. Wraps fs.statSync and fs.readFileSync. Injected into the optimizer's IOptimizerDeps.fileSizes. Also provides siBytesAsTokens(bytes, fileExt) → number (extracted from compiler.ts CHARS_PER_TOKEN). Platform: node.
plugin-io.rationale: IO is the most basic plugin — without it, si_bytes=0 and pairwise_overlap actual stays at 0 (prospective only). Separating it into a plugin lets the optimizer run in browser/memory/test contexts where file I/O is impossible or undesirable.
plugin-io.guard: npx nx build dispatch-plugin-io && npx nx test dispatch-plugin-io

plugin-gitnexus.description: @adhd/dispatch-plugin-gitnexus ships: enrichWithGitnexus(snapshot: DagSnapshot, repoRoot: string): DagSnapshot. Reads the repo via gitnexus MCP to populate: blast_radius[] (upstream consumers per symbol), from/breaking/severity (AST read for current state of each file:symbol), conflict (same-wave op-key collision scan). Returns a new snapshot with these fields enriched. Does NOT modify the optimizer — runs post-snapshot, pre-optimize in the orchestrator pipeline.
plugin-gitnexus.rationale: gitnexus enrichment is the highest-value plugin but the most optional — it requires a gitnexus MCP server running. The optimizer works without it (all enriched fields stay null/[]). Making it a separate plugin keeps the optimizer pristine and lets consumers opt in when gitnexus is available.
plugin-gitnexus.guard: npx nx build dispatch-plugin-gitnexus && npx nx test dispatch-plugin-gitnexus

tools-mcp.description: @adhd/dispatch-tools ships: an MCP server that wraps IDagClient. Tools exposed: dag.milestone_add/get/list/update/delete, dag.operation_add/get/list/update/delete, dag.pending_get/set/clear, dag.field_get/set, dag.dispatch_log_append/get/list, dag.snapshot (read-only summary), dag.validate (structural). Does NOT expose full_dag() to agents — agents get scoped query methods only. Safety: validates after every mutation, atomic writes, no deletion without confirm:true, audit trail via dispatch_log notes on every tool call.
tools-mcp.rationale: D-13 specifies that dag-mutation actions are tool-call operations. This MCP server is the tool-call executor for dag mutations. The critical invariant: no agent ever reads dag.json into its context window. The planning agent calls dag.milestone_add() to author plan structure; the replan agent calls dag.pending_clear() + dag.operation_add() to fill in research results. The tools enforce referential integrity (operations reference existing milestones), structural validity (no cycles), and D-07 eligibility invariants.
tools-mcp.guard: npx nx build dispatch-tools && npx nx test dispatch-tools

serializer-sqlite.description: @adhd/dispatch-serializer-sqlite ships: createSqliteSerializer(db: BetterSQLite3Database) → IDagSerializer. Stores dag.json as a JSON blob in a single-row table. read() queries and parses; write() serializes and upserts; appendDispatchLog() reads, appends, writes atomically in a transaction. Enables embedded use in agent-mcp or browser contexts. Platform: shared (BetterSQLite3 works in both).
serializer-sqlite.rationale: The serializer adapter pattern proves itself here — the same DagClient works identically whether dag.json lives on disk or in SQLite. This enables an agent-mcp instance to manage plans without filesystem access, or a browser-based plan viewer to operate on in-memory dags.
serializer-sqlite.guard: npx nx build dispatch-serializer-sqlite && npx nx test dispatch-serializer-sqlite

orchestrator-core.description: @adhd/dispatch-orchestrator ships: (1) OrchestratorDeps interface — { client: IDagClient, optimizer: { snapshot, optimize }, plugins?: { io?, gitnexus? }, agentMcp?: IDispatchAgentRunner, calibration?: ICalibrationStore }; (2) orchestrateCycle(deps): Promise<CycleResult> — runs one scheduling cycle: client.full_dag() → optimizer.snapshot(dag, resolvedDeps) → apply plugin enrichments → optimizer.optimize(snapshot) → apply sentinel fanout grouping → for each DispatchUnit, resolve provider + create agent + fire task + store remote_task_id; (3) orchestrate(deps): AsyncIterable<CycleResult> — runs cycles until terminal; (4) handle guard-only milestones, replan injection (triggered_by), correction milestones, pending-surfaced gate, dispatch_log appending after each task completes
orchestrator-core.rationale: The orchestrator is the state machine loop — the bridge between the pure optimizer and the real world. It wires: client (for reading/writing dag.json), optimizer (for computing assignments), plugins (for enrichment), agent-mcp (for firing tasks), and calibration (for tracking B values). It's the only package that knows about all of them. The cycle design: snapshot → enrich → optimize → dispatch → poll → record → repeat — is a fixed pipeline that can be paused, resumed, and inspected.
orchestrator-core.guard: npx nx build dispatch-orchestrator && npx nx typecheck dispatch-orchestrator

cli.description: apps/dispatch-cli ships: (1) dispatch snapshot <plan-slug> — client.full_dag() → optimizer.snapshot() + plugin enrichments → write dag-snapshot.json; (2) dispatch optimize <plan-slug> — full compile: snapshot + optimize → print dispatch plan summary; (3) dispatch run <plan-slug> — orchestrator loop, streams progress; (4) dispatch status <plan-slug> — renders open_questions, eligible milestones, wave progress; (5) dispatch calibrate <model-tier> — fires a null-task dispatch via agent-mcp to measure B, writes to ~/.adhd/dispatch-calibration.json; (6) dispatch validate <plan-slug> — validateDagJson() + print errors
cli.rationale: The CLI is the human entrypoint. It wires: JsonFileSerializer → DagClient → (Optimizer + Plugins + AgentMcpRunner) → Orchestrator. Each subcommand is self-contained. The CLI does no business logic — it's a thin command router.

tests-golden.description: tests/goldens/dispatch-production/snapshot.golden.json and dispatch-units.golden.json exist. tests/harness.ts compares current compiler output against goldens — exit 0 on match, exit 1 on divergence. GOLDEN_HISTORY.md records each regen. Covers the full pipeline: dag.json → snapshot() → optimize() → DispatchUnit[] — compared byte-identical on all non-timestamp fields across two runs (snapshot determinism).
tests-golden.rationale: SCOPE.md §F1 defines L1 success: compiler correctness. Golden files are the pre-commit gate. Without them, a refactor of the optimizer can silently change dispatch decisions. The golden is committed alongside code changes — git log on the golden recovers full metric history.

tests-algorithms.description: dispatch-optimizer test suite covers: (1) Bitmask DP — 5 known-optimal fixtures at N=3,5,8,12,15 with verified minimal cost; (2) Tree DP — 3 forest fixtures at N=5,10,20; (3) Simulated Annealing — 3 general DAG fixtures at N=8,15,30, asserting result cost ≤ 1.15 × CP-SAT known optimal; (4) HLFET — 2 large fixtures at N=60,150, asserting all precedence constraints hold and no context window violation; (5) Algorithm selection — N≤20 selects Bitmask DP, forest selects Tree DP, general ≤50 selects SA, >50 selects HLFET; (6) Edge cases: empty plan, single milestone, cycle detection throws, guard-only milestone produces null prompt, all tool-call unit produces null prompt
tests-algorithms.rationale: The 4 algorithms are the optimizer's core value. Zero tests exist. Known-optimal fixtures verify correctness against published solutions (or CP-SAT for SA's tolerance band). Edge cases ensure the system degrades gracefully, never crashes.

backlog-fill.description: All 7 stubs from BL-105 closed: (1) attempt_count counts dispatch_ids; (2) tokens_actual per-op prorated from dispatch turn totals by ki_estimate share; (3) mcp_servers resolved from agent catalog via injected IAgentCatalog interface; (4) blast_radius stubbed as empty (requires gitnexus — covered by plugin-gitnexus); (5) from/breaking/severity stubbed as null (requires AST — covered by plugin-gitnexus); (6) conflict stubbed as empty (requires same-wave scan — covered by plugin-gitnexus); (7) raised_at_dispatch/raised_at_turn populated from dispatch_log entry where pending was first set. BL-106 (b_per_tier cold-start) seeded from SCOPE.md §Open Decisions #2 defaults. BL-107 (backward-compat patches) moved into readDag() normalization pass in serializer-json.
backlog-fill.rationale: These stubs block real dispatch: mcp_servers=null means the orchestrator can't create agent-mcp agent definitions (BL-105 HIGH). Cold-start B values mean tokens_estimated is null on fresh plans (BL-106). Backward-compat in run.ts means every consumer must replicate 50 lines of patches (BL-107). Closing these makes DispatchUnit fully self-contained per D-17.
backlog-fill.guard: npx nx test dispatch-optimizer && npx nx test dispatch-orchestrator

tests-real-e2e.description:
tests/integration/real-e2e.ts exercises the full product lifecycle end-to-end with 8 real-world scenarios — no mocks beyond the agent-mcp task runner boundary. Each scenario is a self-contained test case that creates a dag.json from nothing through a DagClient, drives dispatch via the orchestrator, and asserts consumer-visible outcomes. Every scenario has teeth: reverting the code under test must cause it to fail.

Scenario 1 — Cold start from empty directory: no dag.json exists; dispatch init --plan test-e2e creates a skeleton; dispatch status shows 0 milestones, 0 eligible; exit 0.

Scenario 2 — Author plan via MCP tools: an agent (driven programmatically) calls dag.milestone_add for 3 milestones (research → interface → implement, with depends_on edges), then dag.operation_add for 5 generative ops, then dag.validate → valid: true. The CLI dispatch status now shows 3 milestones, 1 eligible (research), 5 open_questions (none — pending is null on all). Assert the dag.json file on disk is structurally valid (cycle-free, no orphans).

Scenario 3 — Snapshot + optimize on authored plan: the CLI dispatch optimize test-e2e produces DispatchUnit[1] (only research is eligible), prompt is non-null (generative ops), fits_context_window: true, sentinel_role: solo (N=1, no fanout). Assert the compiled prompt contains all 3 milestone descriptions and all 5 op specs. Assert tokens_estimated is a positive integer (B cold-start seeded).

Scenario 4 — Dispatch via orchestrator → agent-mcp: the orchestrator fires the research milestone through a real Haiku agent-mcp dispatch (the 'real model' exception — gated behind DISPATCH_E2E_LIVE=1 env flag documented in README + CLAUDE.md + test file header). The Haiku agent produces a context file at tmp/test-e2e/contexts/embedding-research.md. After the dispatch closes: the milestone status transitions to complete; dispatch_log has 1 new entry with kind:execution, turns populated; a replay of snapshot() shows the milestone eligible=false and status=complete. Assert the dispatched agent's guard command passes.

Scenario 5 — Second cycle: replan triggers: after research completes, snapshot() shows the interface milestone is now eligible (dep satisfied, pending=null). The orchestrator injects no replan milestone because pending was already null at authoring time — the interface milestone was always fully specified. The second cycle dispatches the interface milestone. Assert two dispatch_log entries now, the interface guard passes (npx nx typecheck), and the snapshot shows 2 complete, 1 pending (implementation still blocked on interface).

Scenario 6 — Guard failure and correction injection: the implementation milestone's guard is crafted to fail on first attempt (e.g., test assertion expects a field the AI hasn't authored yet). The orchestrator detects the guard failure, injects a review milestone (triggered_by the failing dispatch), and marks implementation as pending-surfaced. Assert: (a) open_questions[] contains the injected question with surfaced=true; (b) implementation eligibility is false despite deps being complete (guard failed); (c) the review milestone is now eligible and has triggered_by set to the failing dispatch UUID.

Scenario 7 — Correction resolves and retry succeeds: the review milestone dispatches (simple doc op → writes a correction note). A replan clears implementation.pending and adds the missing operation. The orchestrator re-dispatches implementation with the added op. Guard passes. Assert: (a) implementation.status=complete; (b) terminal milestone (the plan itself) is now eligible; (c) dispatch_log contains 4 entries (research, interface, review, implementation) + 1 for the failed attempt; (d) total tokens_actual across all dispatches is recorded and non-zero.

Scenario 8 — CLI resume and status: kill the process mid-cycle. Re-run dispatch run test-e2e. Assert the orchestrator resumes from the last dispatch_log entry — it does NOT re-dispatch completed milestones. dispatch status shows correct progress. dispatch calibrate Sonnet fires a null-task and writes ~/.adhd/dispatch-calibration.json. Assert the file exists and contains a 'Sonnet' key with a positive integer.
tests-real-e2e.rationale: The integration test must prove the system works at the seams that fail in real use — the orchestrator-to-agent-mcp boundary, the replan injection, the guard failure recovery, the process-resume path. Green unit tests on mocked interfaces cannot prove these. Each scenario is a consumer-observable outcome driven through the real entrypoints (dispatch init, dispatch status, dispatch optimize, dispatch run, dispatch calibrate). The only mock is the agent-mcp task boundary (the LLM/code-generation step), and even that gets one live run in scenario 4 behind the documented env gate.
tests-real-e2e.guard: npx nx build dispatch-cli && npx tsx tests/integration/real-e2e.ts

hardening-complete.description: All packages build. All package tests pass (dispatch-spec, dispatch-client, dispatch-optimizer, dispatch-serializer-json, dispatch-serializer-sqlite, dispatch-plugin-io, dispatch-plugin-gitnexus, dispatch-tools, dispatch-orchestrator). Golden files match. Algorithm test suite passes all 5 categories. All 7 BL-105 stubs closed. All 8 real-world e2e scenarios pass (scenario 4 gated behind DISPATCH_E2E_LIVE=1 per the live-test policy). CLI dispatch --help renders. The plan dag.json itself passes strict validation.
hardening-complete.rationale: Terminal validation. Guard-only — all work is done by the preceding milestones. This milestone gates on the union of all package tests + golden tests + algorithm tests + e2e scenarios + structural validation of the plan dag itself.
hardening-complete.guard: node scripts/validate-dag.js --strict docs/plan/dispatch-production/dag.json && npx nx test dispatch-spec && npx nx test dispatch-client && npx nx test dispatch-optimizer && npx nx test dispatch-serializer-json && npx nx test dispatch-serializer-sqlite && npx nx test dispatch-plugin-io && npx nx test dispatch-plugin-gitnexus && npx nx test dispatch-tools && npx nx test dispatch-orchestrator && npx tsx tests/integration/real-e2e.ts

ops.to: byte-identical non-timestamp fields across two snapshot() calls
optimize() output compared against committed golden
hard pass/fail gate — exit 0 on match, 1 on divergence
empty, single, cycle, guard-only, all-tool-call, no-eligible
per-op from dispatch totals by ki_estimate share
from dispatch_log entry where pending first set
cold start, author, snapshot, dispatch, cycle, guard-failure, correction, resume
DISPATCH_E2E_LIVE=1 gated behind env var per CLAUDE.md §6
type union of 48 structural operation literals
interface { op: ShapeOpType; target: string | null; to: string | null; position: number | null; required: boolean | null }
interface extends ShapeOpDag { from, breaking, severity }
interface { kind: 'doc'; description: string; objective: string; required_sections: string[] }
type ShapeCode | ShapeConfig | ShapeDoc | ShapeStructuredOutput | ShapeNull
type ShapeCodeSnapshot | ShapeConfigSnapshot | ShapeDoc | ShapeStructuredOutput | ShapeNull
interface { file: string; symbol: string; impact: 'implements' | 'calls' | 'imports' | 'extends' | 're-exports' | 'overrides'; consumer: 'current' | 'future' }
interface { detected: boolean; competing_op: string | null; op_key: string | null; resolution: 'safe-merge' | 'warning' | 'error' | null }
interface { retries: number; min_timeout: number; max_timeout: number; factor: number }
interface { type: ProviderType; model_id: string; env_secret: string | null; base_url: string | null; timeout_ms: number; retry_config: RetryConfig }
interface { turn: number; input_tokens: number; output_tokens: number; t: string }
interface { op_id: string; status: 'complete' | 'failed' | 'skipped'; guard_result: GuardResult | null; guard_output: string | null; guard_ran_at: string | null }

interface { id: string; kind: DispatchKind; provider: string; model: string | null; agent: string; effort: EffortTier | null; started_at: string; completed_at: string | null; operations: string[]; turns: Turn[]; results: DispatchResult[]; notes: DispatchNote[] }

interface { id, milestone, depends_on, type, action, file, symbol, provenance, confidence, audit_check, criteria, tool, args, guard, to_file, to_symbol, ki_estimate, ki_source, authored_by, status, shape }

interface extends Omit<OperationDag, 'shape'> { shape: ShapeSnapshot | null; dispatch_ids: string[]; attempt_count: number; guard_result/guard_output/guard_ran_at; blast_radius: BlastRadiusEntry[]; conflict: OperationConflict; tokens_actual: number | null }
interface { description, rationale?, authored_by, pending, triggered_by, phase, depends_on, agent, model, effort, two_stage, read_only, guard }
interface { id, text, blocking, surfaced, raised_at_dispatch, raised_at_turn, answered, answer }
interface extends MilestoneDag { context, wave, eligible, status, started_at, completed_at, guard_result, guard_output, artifacts, si_bytes, ki_estimate, tokens_estimated, tokens_actual }
interface { enabled: boolean; write_multiplier: number; read_multiplier: number; hit_probability: number }
interface { sentinel_fanout, b_per_tier, context_window_per_tier, context_window_override, b_override }

interface { schema_version, plan_kind, description, problem, approach, executor, executor_model?, executor_effort?, phases, terminal, assumed_baseline?, cross_plan_deps?, optimization, providers, effort_max_tokens, milestones, operations, dispatch_log }
interface { snapshot_at, snapshot_version, plan, ...DagJson fields, milestones: Record<string, MilestoneSnapshot>, operations: OperationSnapshot[], pairwise_overlap, dispatch_units?, open_questions }

interface { id, milestones, operations, model, effort, two_stage, provider, agent_name, mcp_servers, resolved_max_tokens, background, prompt, context_files, si_bytes, tokens_estimated, fits_context_window, sentinel_role, dispatch_log_id, remote_task_id, result, status, started_at, completed_at, tokens_actual }
interface { path: string; message: string; value?: unknown }
interface { bPerTier, contextWindowPerTier, fileSizes?, readFiles?, dispatchLog? }
eligible milestones must not have pending
function (fromVersion: number, toVersion: number, dag: Record<string, unknown>): void
providers, effort_max_tokens, optimization defaults, op type/normalization
12 cases: valid plan, non-object, missing fields, invalid plan_kind, cycle, missing terminal, orphan op, invalid model, invalid shape kind, missing op dep, cross_plan_deps, empty plan
6 cases: valid snapshot, D-07 violation, invalid status, negative wave, missing fields, dispatch_units present

interface { readonly format: string; read(): Promise<DagJson | null>; write(dag: DagJson): Promise<void>; appendDispatchLog(entry: DispatchLogInput): Promise<void>; onExternalWrite?(listener: () => void): () => void }

interface { milestone_add, milestone_get, milestone_list, milestone_update, milestone_delete, operation_add, operation_get, operation_list, operation_update, operation_delete, pending_set, pending_clear, dispatch_log_append, dispatch_log_get, field_get, field_set, full_dag, validate, create }

function grouping milestones by shape.kind family + model tier
function (N, DAG structure) → BitmaskDP | TreeDP | SA | HLFET

function (slugs, milestones, opsByMilestone, bEff, W): string[][] — exact O(N²W) bottom-up for forest/SP DAGs
function (slugs, milestones, opsByMilestone, bEff, W): string[][] — SA with cooling schedule
function (slugs, milestones, opsByMilestone, bEff, W): string[][] — critical-path priority list O(N log N)

function (bytes: number, filePath?: string): number — chars_per_token lookup
via AST read at HEAD + deterministic lookup table

tools: dag.milestone_add/get/list/update/delete, dag.operation_add/get/list/update/delete, dag.pending_get/set/clear, dag.field_get/set, dag.dispatch_log_append/get/list, dag.snapshot (summary only), dag.validate

operations.guard: node scripts/validate-dag.js --strict docs/plan/dispatch-production/dag.json && npx nx test dispatch-spec && npx nx test dispatch-client && npx nx test dispatch-optimizer && npx nx test dispatch-serializer-json && npx nx test dispatch-serializer-sqlite && npx nx test dispatch-plugin-io && npx nx test dispatch-plugin-gitnexus && npx nx test dispatch-tools && npx nx test dispatch-orchestrator && npx tsx tests/integration/real-e2e.ts
